calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar May-02-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 304

ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-25-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Set For Trial On May-05-25 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a).

1. The trial date is May 5, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is May 14, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 29, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is April 18, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is May 2, 2025.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 13, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(Mini minutes continue in next entry)

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2)
(Mini minutes continue from previous entry)

8. No later than January 28, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.

9. A further case management conference is set for March 18, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than March 31, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due April 14, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on April 21, 2025.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, May 1, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not Reported, Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
